headline: J&J enters US robotic surgery market after device gets marketing authorization
summary: Johnson & Johnson said on Wednesday the U.S. Food and Drug Administration had granted marketing authorization for its robotic surgery ‌device, as the healthcare conglomerate enters the soft-tissue robotic surgery market.  The decision ‌marks a key step for J&J's medtech business that had been seeking to compete in the robotic-assisted ​surgery market, currently dominated by Intuitive Surgical's da Vinci system.  Medtronic's Hugo robot was also approved last year.

headline: J&J gets FDA authorization for Ottava robot, teeing up competition with Intuitive
summary: While Johnson & Johnson will go head to head with Intuitive in the U.S., analysts questioned whether its robotic surgery system will take meaningful share from the market leader.

headline: Medtronic (MDT) Unveils Touch Surgery Aide For Real Time AI In Operating Rooms
summary: Medtronic (NYSE:MDT) has introduced Touch Surgery Aide, a next generation real-time AI platform designed for operating rooms. The platform supports AI driven surgical decision assistance for robotic assisted procedures. Its first live AI application, Instrument Exit Point (IEP), has received FDA clearance, signaling clinical and regulatory progress. Medtronic enters this launch with its stock at $82.35 and mixed recent performance. The shares are up 3.8% over the past week and 3.8% over the...
